notes.anchor: TVLLIVS ET CICERO S. D. TIRONI SVO.
notes.note: Senders are Cicero ('Tullius') and his son young Marcus ('Cicero'); 'S. D.' = salutem dicit, 'sends greetings'; 'suo' ('his own') marks the warmth toward Tiro.
Corcyra (modern Corfu), the large island off the coast of Epirus where ships waited for favourable winds before crossing to Italy.

notes.anchor: Quintus my brother and his son at Buthrotum
notes.note: Cicero's younger brother Quintus and Quintus's son (Cicero's nephew) had stopped at Buthrotum, a town on the Epirote coast facing Corcyra where Atticus held property.

notes.anchor: The fifteenth day before the Kalends of December
notes.note: 17 November 50 BC. The Kalends are the 1st of the month; Roman counting back from the Kalends of December is inclusive.
